description: Baseline audit found one concrete security issue (Claude dangerous-mode prompt bypass), but full chezmoi apply validation timed out and needs a faster check path.

asi.hypothesis: Baseline the current agent-safety audit before changing repo docs or client settings.
asi.rollback_reason: Validation strategy was too heavy for an autoresearch loop because chezmoi apply exceeded the checks timeout.
asi.next_action_hint: Replace the slow validation step with a lighter but still principled check (JSON parse plus targeted chezmoi render/status verification) before trying repo changes.
asi.insight: The initial audit already exposed a real low-hanging security improvement; the bottleneck is validation, not idea generation.

description: Disable Claude's tracked dangerous-mode prompt bypass and add concise guardrails in CLAUDE.md plus the repo-maintainer skill so safety-bypass settings stay opt-in and local-only.

asi.hypothesis: Removing the committed Claude permission-bypass plus adding small guidance guardrails will eliminate the low-hanging safety finding without widening scope.
asi.insight: A single client-config default (`skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`) was the clearest low-risk security win. Minimal docs/skill reinforcement helps prevent regressions even though the main metric was driven by the config fix.
asi.guardrail: Keep approval/prompt bypasses out of tracked client configs; if needed, use machine-local untracked overrides instead.
asi.scope: Touched only Claude client config, Claude-facing repo guidance, and the cross-cutting repo-maintainer skill.
asi.validation: JSON parse + targeted chezmoi dry-run for rendered Claude settings and shared skill path passed.

name: Agent prompt and skill guidance low-hanging improvements audit

description: Baseline for the broadened prompt/skill audit found two remaining low-hanging guidance issues: missing Claude first-pass workflow cues and a weak repo-maintainer Codex metadata prompt.

asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline after broadening the audit from client-config safety into prompt/skill guidance quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline only; no repo changes beyond autoresearch scaffolding should be kept from this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a concise First Pass section to CLAUDE.md and strengthen the repo-maintainer openai metadata prompt with planning and validation reminders.
asi.insight: The next improvements are lightweight documentation/prompt nudges rather than behavior changes, so targeted edits should be sufficient.

description: Add a concise First Pass checklist to CLAUDE.md and strengthen the repo-maintainer Codex metadata prompt with read-order, planning, and chezmoi validation reminders.

asi.hypothesis: A small Claude-first-pass section plus stronger repo-maintainer metadata prompting will remove the remaining guidance findings with minimal doc churn.
asi.insight: The most effective low-hanging prompt improvements were not longer docs, but short high-leverage reminders placed directly in the tool-specific surfaces agents actually load early.
asi.validation: Targeted chezmoi dry-run for the rendered skill metadata path and Claude settings path passed; no behavior or bootstrap scripts changed.
asi.best_practice: Mirror the AGENTS first-pass workflow in tool-specific prompts so individual clients don't rely on implicit inheritance from repo-root docs alone.

name: Agent config health-check low-hanging improvements audit

description: Baseline for agent-config health-check coverage found two low-hanging gaps: no check for Claude's dangerous-mode prompt setting and no shared-skill-routing verification for Codex/Claude.

asi.hypothesis: Broaden the audit from docs/prompt guidance into durable operational checks inside chezmoi-health-check.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline only; no repo changes beyond autoresearch instrumentation should be kept from this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a small Agent Configuration section to chezmoi-health-check that validates shared skill-tree routing and warns if Claude bypasses dangerous-mode confirmation.
asi.insight: The next best improvement is to turn the earlier documentation/security work into runtime checks so local drift and regressions become visible.

description: Add an Agent Configuration section to chezmoi-health-check so it verifies shared skill routing for Codex/Claude and warns if Claude enables the dangerous-mode permission bypass.

asi.hypothesis: A small operational health-check addition can turn the earlier prompt/security fixes into durable runtime validation with minimal script churn.
asi.insight: For this repo, the best low-hanging follow-up after docs/prompt fixes was a lightweight operational check: it catches local drift and makes regressions visible without touching bootstrap behavior.
asi.validation: bash -n passed and chezmoi dry-run for ~/.local/bin/chezmoi-health-check plus rendered agent paths passed.
asi.best_practice: When a client-config safety default matters, back it with a cheap health-check instead of relying on docs alone.

name: Agent safety docs discoverability low-hanging improvements audit

description: Baseline for agent-safety docs discoverability found two small gaps: README does not mention chezmoi-health-check, and docs/tooling-and-skills lacks a canonical cross-tool note about local-only client-config safety bypasses.

asi.hypothesis: After improving runtime checks, the next low-hanging win is making those safeguards discoverable in user-facing and canonical docs.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline only; no repo changes beyond autoresearch instrumentation should be kept from this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Add chezmoi-health-check to the README command table and add a short cross-tool client-config safety/defaults note to docs/tooling-and-skills.md.
asi.insight: The remaining improvements are documentation discoverability gaps, not code behavior changes.
